summary: Generalized myoclonic seizures present with bilateral, synchronous jerking of upper and lower limbs and occasional neck twitches—matching the described findings. Among given options, only "multifocal clonic" accounts for bilaterality while avoiding localized onset, making it the most appropriate selection despite its imprecision.
